example_summary: During the total synthesis of rhizoxin D, an asymmetric aldol reaction was utilized to couple two key fragments. The reaction involves an aldehyde and a chiral enolate derived from a ketone using (+)-chlorodiisopinocampheylborane, affording the product with a high diastereomeric ratio of 17-20:1.

extracts.notes_text: diastereomeric ratio: 17-20:1. This is a “matched” aldol reaction where the stereochemical induction of the chiral boron substituent and the stereocenters present in the enolate reinforce each other.
